title: lithocardium mariquitense
intro: Lithocardium mariquitense, also known as Lithraea brasiliensis, is a plant species native to South America, primarily found in the tropical forests of Brazil. The plant's bark contains lithospermic acid, a compound that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lithocardium mariquitense include reducing blood pressure and alleviating symptoms of hyperlipidemia, making it a potential treatment for cardiovascular diseases. Herbal preparations of Lithocardium mariquitense are typically made from the dried and crushed bark, which can be steeped in hot water to create a tea or decoction for medicinal use.
